title: micromeria graeca subsp. garganica
intro: Micromeria graeca subsp. garganica, also known as Garganic mint or small leafed thyme, is a subspecies of the Micromeria graeca plant, a type of low-growing, woody, evergreen shrub in the mint family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a carminative and antiseptic, to relieve digestive issues and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Micromeria graeca subsp. garganica include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions, decoctions, or essential oil extractions, which can be used to treat a variety of health conditions, from digestive issues to skin problems.
